question:
I'm new to this codebase and need a real code-based tour before my first behavior change.

Find the central runtime flow, trace it from the public API to the code that does the work, and show the nearby code paths and tests I should read next.

shellCommands: "C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"SECTION\\(\" tests\\src\\unit-deserialization.cpp tests\\src\\unit-serialization.cpp tests\\src\\unit-class_parser.cpp tests\\src\\unit-class_lexer.cpp tests\\src\\unit-diagnostics.cpp | Select-String -Pattern \"deserialization|serialization|parser class|lexer class|successful deserialization|Parse error|comments|trailing commas|dump with binary values|token_type_name|parse errors on first character|fail on comments|ignore comments\""

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"TEST_CASE\\(\\\"lexer class\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"scan\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"token_type_name\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"parse errors on first character\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"fail on comments\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"ignore comments\\\"\" tests\\src\\unit-class_lexer.cpp"

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"TEST_CASE\\(\\\"deserialization\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"successful deserialization\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"unsuccessful deserialization\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"contiguous containers\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"JSON Lines\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"SAX and early abort\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"Using _json with char8_t literals #4945\\\"\" tests\\src\\unit-deserialization.cpp"

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"TEST_CASE\\(\\\"serialization\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"dump\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"dump with binary values\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"round-trip dump/parse\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"dump for basic_json with long double number_float_t\\\"\" tests\\src\\unit-serialization.cpp"

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"parser_helper\\(|accept_helper\\(|comments_helper\\(|trailing_comma_helper\\(|TEST_CASE\\(\\\"parser class\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"parse errors\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"parse errors \\(accept\\)\\\"|SECTION\\(\\\"error messages for comments\\\"\" tests\\src\\unit-class_parser.cpp"
baseline.answer:
The runtime hub is [`basic_json`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L127). `json` is just an alias for that type in [`json_fwd.hpp`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json_fwd.hpp#L62), so the real behavior lives in `basic_json` and the `detail::` helpers it delegates to.

**Input Flow**
- Public entry points are [`basic_json::parse`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L4090), [`accept`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L4133), and [`sax_parse`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L4163).
- The stream operators are thin wrappers: [`operator<<`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L4050) serializes, and [`operator>>`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L4227) parses.
- Those APIs normalize input through [`detail::input_adapter`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/input_adapters.hpp#L495), which turns strings, iterators, streams, and files into a common byte source.
- The parser itself is [`detail::parser`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/parser.hpp#L60). Its constructor primes the lexer, `parse()` builds a DOM, `accept()` just validates, and `sax_parse()` streams events.
- The tokenization work happens in [`detail::lexer`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/lexer.hpp#L152), with the main dispatch in `scan()` around [`lexer.hpp:1610`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/lexer.hpp#L1610).
- DOM construction happens through [`json_sax_dom_parser`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/json_sax.hpp#L167); validation-only parsing uses [`json_sax_acceptor`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/json_sax.hpp#L910).
- If your change touches parse callbacks, the adjacent path is [`json_sax_dom_callback_parser`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/json_sax.hpp#L473).
- If your change touches non-JSON binary formats, `sax_parse(...)` branches to `detail::binary_reader` from the same public API in [`json.hpp`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L4163).

**Output Flow**
- Public serialization is [`basic_json::dump`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L1338).
- The actual work is [`detail::serializer`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/output/serializer.hpp#L51), and the recursive `switch` over `value_t` starts at [`serializer.hpp:107`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/output/serializer.hpp#L107).
- `dump()` writes through the output adapter layer, so formatting, escaping, and UTF-8 handling all live under that serializer/output-adapter boundary.

**What To Read Next**
- [`tests/src/unit-class_parser.cpp`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-class_parser.cpp#L217) is the best first read for the parser internals. Start at `parser_helper`, `accept_helper`, `comments_helper`, and `trailing_comma_helper`, then read the main [`TEST_CASE("parser class")`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-class_parser.cpp#L349).
- [`tests/src/unit-deserialization.cpp`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-deserialization.cpp#L237) exercises the public surface. The useful sections are `successful deserialization`, `unsuccessful deserialization`, `contiguous containers`, `JSON Lines`, `SAX and early abort`, and `Using _json with char8_t literals #4945`.
- [`tests/src/unit-class_lexer.cpp`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-class_lexer.cpp#L35) is the token-level contract. Read `scan`, `token_type_name`, `parse errors on first character`, `fail on comments`, and `ignore comments`.
- [`tests/src/unit-serialization.cpp`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-serialization.cpp#L18) covers the output side. The most relevant sections are `dump`, `dump with binary values`, `round-trip dump/parse`, and `dump for basic_json with long double number_float_t`.
- [`tests/src/unit-diagnostics.cpp`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-diagnostics.cpp#L20) is the place to check when behavior changes affect error text, JSON Pointer paths, or diagnostic positions.
- For the architecture overview that matches these code paths, skim [`docs/mkdocs/docs/home/architecture.md`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/docs/mkdocs/docs/home/architecture.md#L8), especially the `Parsing inputs` and `Writing outputs` sections.

**Practical reading order**
- [`docs/mkdocs/docs/home/architecture.md`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/docs/mkdocs/docs/home/architecture.md#L8)

- [`include/nlohmann/detail/input/input_adapters.hpp`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/input_adapters.hpp#L495)
- [`include/nlohmann/detail/input/parser.hpp`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/parser.hpp#L60)
- [`include/nlohmann/detail/input/lexer.hpp`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/lexer.hpp#L152)
- [`include/nlohmann/detail/input/json_sax.hpp`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/json_sax.hpp#L167)
- [`tests/src/unit-class_parser.cpp`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-class_parser.cpp#L217)
- [`tests/src/unit-deserialization.cpp`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-deserialization.cpp#L237)
- [`include/nlohmann/json.hpp`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L1338)
- [`include/nlohmann/detail/output/serializer.hpp`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/output/serializer.hpp#L51)
- [`tests/src/unit-serialization.cpp`](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-serialization.cpp#L18)

If you want, I can turn this into a narrower “change map” next, for example:
1. parse errors and diagnostics
2. UTF-8 and dumping
3. JSON Pointer / update / mutation paths
